title: 2026 Vermont Governor Election
description: The 2026 Vermont governor’s race is for the state’s chief executive office, with Republican incumbent Phil Scott seeking reelection and Democrat Amanda Janoo mounting an early challenge. The contest is likely to center on affordability, education governance, taxes, and Scott’s long tenure, with Janoo framing her campaign around housing, health care, and opposition to forced school consolidation.

polling.pollster: University of New Hampshire Survey Center

polling_note: No polls published after 2026-04-01T06:08:24.063764+00:00 were found in the available sources. The most recent poll currently in the profile predates that cutoff.

candidates.name: Phil Scott
candidates.summary: Phil Scott is the Republican incumbent governor of Vermont and is seeking reelection in 2026. Recent coverage has centered on his push to overhaul Vermont’s education governance and funding system, including school consolidation proposals, while he continues to emphasize affordability and state management.

career_history.title: Governor of Vermont
career_history.description: Serves as Vermont's chief executive; reelected to a fifth term in 2024.

career_history.title: Lieutenant Governor of Vermont
career_history.description: Served three terms before becoming governor.

career_history.title: Vermont State Senator
career_history.description: Represented Washington County; served as vice chair of the Transportation Committee and chair of the Institutions Committee.

career_history.title: Co-owner
career_history.description: Co-owned his family's construction business for more than 30 years.

candidates.name: Amanda Janoo
candidates.summary: Amanda Janoo is a Democratic candidate for governor who announced her campaign in March 2026. Her launch focused on affordability, housing, free primary and preventive health care, and opposition to forced school consolidation, and she has said she will seek the Progressive Party’s endorsement.
